schema_evolution_summary.recommendation: Breaking change detected for fields that active registered consumers depend on. Deployment should be blocked.
producer_next_actions: Block producer deployment until impacted consumers have a migration plan.
Update contract_registry/subscriptions.yaml with any new field names, aliases, or migration notes before release.
Publish a compatibility notice to affected subscribers and regenerate the contract snapshot after the schema fix or migration.
Prefer deprecating the old field with an alias period before removing or renaming it outright.

changes.rationale: Field rename is breaking because existing consumers depend on the previous field path.

breaking_fields.reason: Confidence scale drives trust weighting and graph annotation quality in downstream cartography.
Stable document identity is required to link extraction outputs to lineage or graph nodes.
The generator depends on the nested fact structure to flatten the dataset and infer fact-level clauses.
Range and drift clauses are generated from the observed fact confidence distribution.
Confidence is a critical semantic field and should block on range or high-severity drift in enforce mode.
Hash integrity is used as part of the contract's structural guarantees.

embedding_drift.summary: Semantic drift between the current Week 3 fact text slice and the persisted embedding baseline.
notes: This metric used a real local embedding provider, so the drift score reflects semantic similarity rather than token hashing alone.
Embedding baseline was created from the current Week 3 extraction slice before evaluating drift.

prompt_input_schema_validation.summary: Trace prompt inputs were validated against the observed structured prompt families in the LangSmith export.

llm_output_schema_validation.summary: Week 2 verdict records were checked for stable structured LLM output envelopes.

normalization.summary: Internal helper spans were normalized to documented contract run types while preserving the original raw run_type in metadata.

trace_contract_risk.summary: LangSmith trace telemetry was normalized at the consumer boundary and then checked against the documented Week 7 trace contract expectations.

known_limitations: Week 4 lineage is canonical and useful now, but the consumer-side enrichment is still dominated by dynamic file-I/O observations rather than a clean explicit Week 3 contract edge.

recommended_actions: Keep consumer-side validation in AUDIT first for any new subscriber, then move to WARN or ENFORCE after a clean baseline is established.
Keep SchemaEvolutionAnalyzer in the producer CI path so breaking schema changes are blocked before consumers see them.
AI-specific contract metrics are stable on the current artifacts; keep tracing and schema snapshots in place as the next safety layer.
Block producer deployment until impacted consumers have a migration plan.
Update contract_registry/subscriptions.yaml with any new field names, aliases, or migration notes before release.
Publish a compatibility notice to affected subscribers and regenerate the contract snapshot after the schema fix or migration.
Prefer deprecating the old field with an alias period before removing or renaming it outright.
